summary.en: The city's oldest barrio, defined by faded colonial grandeur, cobblestone streets, and a palpable sense of history that feels both romantic and a little rough around the edges.
The city's nerve center, with majestic Belle Époque architecture and wide avenues, hiding a world of architectural oddities and subterranean secrets.
A cool, unpretentious neighborhood where locals go out, featuring an incredible culinary scene amidst traditional apartment buildings and leather outlet shops.
A relaxed, village-like hub for artists and designers with leafy streets, low-rise buildings, and a growing number of exceptional, design-forward restaurants.
A peaceful, residential area defined by its cobblestone streets and mature trees, offering a quiet retreat with a handful of exceptional, locally-loved restaurants.
A trendy, sophisticated, and design-conscious hub with top restaurants, chic cocktail bars, and independent design boutiques.

description.en: A cozy and modern wine bar perfect for decompressing with a glass of Pinot Noir and a well-curated cheese plate after a day of walking.
A spectacular museum built above excavated underground tunnels from the 17th and 18th centuries, offering a literal descent into the city's history.
A beautifully restored 'Bar Notable' that embodies classic porteño cafe culture, perfect for a coffee and medialuna or a late-afternoon vermouth.
A traditional tavern transported from northern Spain, offering an excellent, no-frills choice for a group lunch of authentic Spanish cuisine.
An architectural marvel designed as an allegory for Dante's Divine Comedy, culminating in a lighthouse with 360-degree city views.
A historic Jesuit complex that serves as a gateway to a mysterious network of 18th-century underground tunnels used for defense and smuggling.
A beloved institution for Armenian food, perpetually packed with locals for its outstanding quality and fair prices.
The city's epicenter for leather goods, where you can see artisans at work and find high-quality jackets, bags, and shoes.
A beautifully designed, laid-back wine bar with a focus on natural and low-intervention Argentine wines and warm, knowledgeable service.
A creative community space and wine bar that often hosts craft nights like ceramics or embroidery workshops paired with wine tastings.
A stunning vegetarian restaurant in a sprawling, plant-filled garden that feels like a secret oasis, serving flavorful wood-fired dishes.
A vast and architecturally diverse cemetery, the final resting place of tango icon Carlos Gardel and countless other cultural figures.
A wonderfully eccentric wine bar in a beautiful old mansion where you can get a tarot card reading while sipping on a boutique Malbec.
A quietly excellent fixture on a picturesque cobblestone street, serving an inventive, ever-changing menu of Argentine-Asian fusion dishes.
An 'experimental' Argentine restaurant with a strong focus on seasonal, organic ingredients, many from its own garden.
A small, whimsical park dedicated entirely to the world of Mafalda, with themed playground equipment, benches, and murals.
A romantic, artsy wine bar that feels like a secret library, with comfy sofas, live jazz, and a focus on boutique Argentine vineyards.
An elegant reimagining of the traditional Argentine barbecue as a nine-course tasting menu served at a communal bar surrounding the grill.
A museum dedicated to a surrealist painter and esoteric thinker, designed by the artist himself to be a journey into a brilliantly eccentric mind.
A short and impossibly charming cobblestone alley that feels like a secret European lane, offering a quiet escape from the bustle.
A beloved statue of the famous comic strip character Mafalda, sitting on a bench in her creator's neighborhood of San Telmo.
The city's main square and a hub of political life in Argentina since the 1810 May Revolution that led to independence.
The iconic 'Pink House' located on the east side of Plaza de Mayo, serving as the official seat of the executive branch of the Argentine government.
The main Catholic church in Buenos Aires, which has stood on the north side of Plaza de Mayo since colonial times.
An internationally rated opera house with stunning architecture, considered one of the best in the world for its acoustics.
A beautiful shopping center located at the intersection of Florida Street and Córdoba Avenue, known for its magnificent painted ceilings.
A classic heladeria on the famous Avenida Corrientes, noted for its good pistachio gelato, though it's one of the more expensive options in the city.
A wine bar with a strong list of by-the-glass options and an extensive charcuterie menu to pair with your drinks.
This spot looks like a craft beer bar from the outside, but its taps actually serve different varieties of wine.
A fun wine bar with a self-service model using wine dispensers and friendly sommeliers available to help you choose.
A sunny and airy wine bar in Palermo Soho, perfect for a tasting flight and a cheese plate after a day of exploring.
A massive urban park designed in the late 19th century, home to lakes, a famous rose garden (El Rosedal), and the planetarium.
The largest Japanese garden of its type outside of Japan, offering a tranquil escape with traditional landscaping, a lake, and a tea house.
A high-quality artisanal heladeria praised for its excellent pistachio and creative flavors like mascarpone with figs.
A famous chocolate shop and heladeria chain with one of the best pistachio flavors in the city and refreshing sorbets like Maracuyá y Kiwi.

prompt.en: Find a cobblestone street and take a low-angle photo that emphasizes its texture and history.
Capture an image of a colonial building's facade, focusing on details that show its age and faded beauty.
Listen for the sound of tango music. Take a photo of the place it's coming from—a bar, a window, or street performers.
Photograph a fascinating object you see through the window of an antique shop.
Peer through an open doorway and find a hidden patio or courtyard. Snap a photo of this secret space.
Take a selfie where the background looks like it could be from a century ago.
Find a piece of modern street art on a crumbling, historic wall and capture the contrast.
Photograph a scene that feels authentically local, like neighbors chatting on a balcony or a dog sleeping in a doorway.
Create a photo collage of three different colorful doors or windows you find in the neighborhood.
Capture an interesting shadow cast by a wrought-iron balcony or lamp post on a cobblestone street.
Take a photo of a classic cafe scene, focusing on the details like the cups, tables, or patrons.
Find and photograph a sign painted in the traditional, colorful 'fileteado' style of Buenos Aires.
Take a photo that captures the romantic but slightly melancholic vibe of the neighborhood.
Order a classic Argentine drink (like wine or vermouth) and take a picture of it with a historic backdrop.
Find an antique or craft item in a shop that you think perfectly represents San Telmo and take a photo of it.
Look up! Take a photo of the top of a grand Belle Époque building against the sky.
Take a selfie in front of a government building that makes you look powerful and important.
Find an imposing or mysterious-looking doorway and take a photo. What secrets could be behind it?
Find a classic 'Bar Notable' and take a photo of a vermouth, a coffee, or a medialuna on a dark wood table.
Photograph one of the wide avenues from a perspective that makes it look like a canyon of grand buildings.
Capture the reflection of a historic building in a modern glass window or a puddle.
Find a subway entrance ('Subte') and take a photo looking down into the darkness, hinting at the city's underground world.
Capture a scene that shows the contrast between the formal architecture and the bustling, everyday life of the street.
Scan the buildings around you and find a strange or unique architectural detail. Take a close-up shot.
Find a quiet plaza or bench amidst the hustle and bustle and take a photo that feels peaceful.
Use a slow shutter speed to capture the motion blur of traffic or pedestrians against the static, grand buildings.
Find a statue of a historical figure and take a creative portrait of it.
Look up and photograph the most beautiful or interesting dome (cúpula) you can find.
Find a bookstore with a classic, old-fashioned storefront and take a picture of its window display.
Find a Spanish restaurant or cultural center and take a photo that captures its Iberian influence.
Order a meal or a collection of small plates and take a top-down 'flat lay' photo of the delicious spread.
Find a bar that serves wine and take a photo that highlights how it's served—from a tap, a bottle, or a carafe.
Find a leather shop and take a photo that contrasts the texture of the leather goods with the gritty street art outside.
Take a selfie that captures the cool, authentic, and non-touristy vibe of the neighborhood.
Photograph a typical residential street corner, capturing the mix of apartment buildings and local shops.
Find a cafe or bar that looks packed with locals and take a discreet photo of the lively atmosphere.
Look for a workshop or open kitchen where you can see an artisan or chef at work. Capture a photo of their hands in action.
Find a restaurant that looks unassuming from the outside but has a menu that intrigues you. Photograph its entrance.
Try to capture a photo that evokes the smell of food—steam rising from a plate, a baker at an oven, or spices in a shop.
Take a picture that finds beauty in the neighborhood's slightly gritty, authentic character.
Find a sign, menu, or dish that points to the neighborhood's Armenian or Middle Eastern culinary influence.
Take a photo of your drink held up with a cool, local street scene in the background.
Photograph the storefront of an outlet shop (leather or otherwise) that looks like a great bargain.
Many great spots here have a line. Capture the sense of anticipation by photographing people waiting for a table at a popular restaurant.

title.en: A Toast to a New Grape

prompt.en: Try a glass of an Argentine grape you've never had before (like Bonarda or Criolla) and take a photo of it.
Find a building that looks like an artist's studio, a design shop, or a creative workshop and photograph its exterior.
Take a relaxed selfie on a quiet, leafy street that captures the neighborhood's calm, village-like atmosphere.
Explore the streets (or the cemetery) and find a building with a striking Art Deco architectural detail. Take a close-up shot.
Find a restaurant or cafe with a beautiful garden or patio. Capture the lush greenery.
Photograph a scene that embodies minimalist design—clean lines, natural light, and simple materials in a local bar or shop.
Find a bar specializing in natural or orange wines. Take a photo of the bottle or your glass, highlighting its unique color.
Find a reference to Carlos Gardel (a street name, a plaque, a mural) and take a photo.
Capture the tranquility of Chacarita by taking a photo of an empty, tree-lined street.
Order a vegetarian dish and take a photo that showcases its creative and artful presentation.
Find a beautiful or unusual sculpture on a mausoleum in the Chacarita Cemetery and take a photo of it.
Find a space that hosts workshops or community events. Take a photo of a sign or flyer advertising one.
Take a photo that shows the neighborhood's characteristic low-rise buildings and spacious feel.
At dusk, capture the warm glow of string lights on a restaurant patio.
Find a sign of the neighborhood's growth—a new restaurant under construction, a freshly painted mural, or a trendy new shop.
The area near the cemetery has many flower shops (floristrias). Capture a photo of the most vibrant floral display you can find.
Take a photo down a quiet, cobblestoned street, framed by the mature trees that line it.
Take a selfie that makes it look like you live here—relaxed, happy, and far from the tourist crowds.
Find a quiet neighborhood plaza and take a photo of people enjoying a peaceful moment.
Find a piece of whimsical street art or a quirky detail on a house that reminds you of a comic strip.
Find a restaurant with its own herb or vegetable garden. Take a photo of the fresh ingredients growing.
Peer into a bar or cafe and capture a photo of a unique or eccentric interior detail, like vintage decor or tarot cards.
Photograph a house or apartment building that you find particularly beautiful or charming.
Order a dish at a local restaurant and take a close-up photo that highlights its beautiful and inventive plating.
Look up and take a photo of the canopy of leaves formed by the mature trees over a street.
Find a small shop or cafe that clearly looks like a beloved local spot and photograph its storefront.
Take a photo of your drink (wine, coffee, etc.) that has a mystical or magical feel to it.
Find a mural or piece of playground equipment inspired by the Mafalda comic strip and take a playful photo with it.
Find a menu that combines Argentine ingredients with another cuisine (like Asian fusion) and snap a picture of it.
Find a picturesque bench in a park or on a sidewalk and take a photo from the perspective of someone sitting there.
Find a beautiful old mansion that has been converted into a business (like a bar or shop) and photograph its historic facade.
Find a storefront, cafe, or restaurant with impeccable interior or exterior design and capture its chic aesthetic.
Take a selfie that makes you look trendy and sophisticated, as if you're about to be discovered by a film director.
Take an artful photo of a sophisticated cocktail at a chic bar.
Inspired by Xul Solar, find something strange or surreal on the street and take a photo of it.
Find a charming 'pasaje' (alley) and take a photo that captures its secret, European feel.
Find a restaurant with an open grill ('parrilla') and capture a dramatic photo of the chefs, the fire, and the food.
Photograph the window display of an independent design boutique that catches your eye.
Find a bar with live music and take a moody, low-light photo that captures the atmosphere.
Find a sign or mural related to film, television, or radio studios in the area.
Capture a candid photo of the stylish people at a popular outdoor cafe or restaurant.
Find a cozy corner in a wine bar and take a photo that looks like the perfect spot for a long, intimate conversation.
Find some interesting text or script (graffiti, a sign, a menu) and photograph it as if it were an invented language.
Take a photo of a tasting menu or a dish that is presented as a full culinary experience, not just a meal.
Many trendy spots have vertical gardens or walls covered in ivy. Find one and take a picture.
Find a wine list and take a photo of a wine made by a female winemaker or from a female-owned vineyard.
Take an artistic, low-angle shot of San Telmo's iconic cobblestone streets. Try to capture the texture and history in the stones.
Find the most interesting or unusual antique in a shop window or at the street market. Snap a photo of this piece of history.
Capture a moment of tango. It could be street performers, a mural, or even just a sign for a milonga. Feel the passion!
Photograph a beautifully preserved colonial facade. Focus on the colors, the ironwork balconies, and the classic architecture.
Find a piece of street art or a mural that tells a story. Take a picture and imagine what the artist was trying to say.
Take a photo of a delicious-looking food item from a stall in the San Telmo Market. What local flavor did you discover?
Capture a reflection of San Telmo's historic buildings in a puddle, a shop window, or a pair of sunglasses.
Take a selfie that captures the bohemian, artistic vibe of San Telmo. Pose with some street art or in a quirky alleyway.
Find and photograph an old, ornate doorway. Imagine all the people who have passed through it over the centuries.
Find a charming corner café and take a photo that makes you want to sit down for a coffee and watch the world go by.
Spot and photograph an example of 'fileteado,' the traditional colorful, stylized art form of Buenos Aires, often seen on signs or old buses.
Take a photo of a street musician or performer adding to the lively atmosphere of San Telmo.
Peer into an open doorway and capture a glimpse of a hidden colonial courtyard (a 'patio').
Photograph the interesting shadows cast by the old buildings and iron balconies on the cobblestone streets.
Respectfully capture a photo that represents the unique characters of San Telmo, whether it's a shopkeeper, an artist, or a long-time resident.
Find a bar advertising Argentina's unofficial national drink, Fernet con Coca. Snap a pic of the sign or a bottle.
Take a photo of an interesting display in one of the many antique shop windows.
Take a selfie with a classic San Telmo backdrop: a cobblestone street, a colonial building, or the bustling market.
Look up! Capture the magnificent facade of one of El Centro's grand, European-style buildings.
Take a photo that captures the constant motion of El Centro—a blur of buses, a crowd of pedestrians, or the flow of traffic on a wide avenue.
Take a selfie with a significant historical landmark in the background, like the Obelisco, Casa Rosada, or the Cabildo.
Photograph a classic 'Café Notable' (historic cafe). Capture the timeless atmosphere of locals enjoying a coffee.
Find a government building and take a photo that conveys a sense of power or history.
Capture the bright lights and posters of the theaters along Avenida Corrientes.
Avenida Corrientes is famous for its bookstores. Snap a photo of a window display or a stack of books that catches your eye.
Find a classic pizzeria and take a mouth-watering photo of a slice of 'fugazzeta' or 'muzzarella'.
Find a building or a street with strong symmetrical lines and take a perfectly balanced photo.
Spot the light blue and white Argentine flag flying proudly and capture it against the city skyline.
Capture a photo of a street performer on the pedestrian Florida Street.
Photograph a scene that shows a contrast between old and new architecture in El Centro.
Take a photo of a classic Buenos Aires kiosk, packed with candy, drinks, and magazines. Capture the colorful chaos.
Take a photo that shows the immense scale of Avenida 9 de Julio, one of the widest avenues in the world.
Capture the hustle and bustle of the 'Microcentro' (financial district) during a weekday.
Find an interesting statue or monument and take a photo from a creative angle.
Capture a classic porteño breakfast: a coffee and a medialuna (croissant). Find a cafe and snap a pic before you indulge.
Find a modern glass building and capture the reflection of a historic building in its facade.
Villa Crespo is famous for its leather outlets. Take a photo of a display of leather jackets, bags, or shoes in a shop window.
Find a storefront with a big 'Oferta' or 'Liquidación' sign. Capture the spirit of bargain hunting in the outlet district.
Find a classic, unassuming neighborhood restaurant (a 'bodegón') and take a photo of its facade. Capture the authentic, local feel.
Wander the side streets and find a cool piece of street art. Villa Crespo has many hidden gems.
The neighborhood is full of new wine bars. Take a photo of one that looks inviting, focusing on the bottles or the cozy interior.
Take a picture of a typical residential street in Villa Crespo, showing the mix of houses and low-rise apartment buildings.
Find a tree-lined street or a small plaza and capture a moment of nature amidst the urban environment.
Take a selfie that shows you're in a real, lived-in Buenos Aires neighborhood, away from the main tourist track.
Photograph the facade of a traditional local business, like a hardware store ('ferretería') or a bakery ('panadería').
Villa Crespo has a rich Jewish history. Find a sign, a building, or a shop that hints at this cultural heritage.
Capture an image that shows the contrast between an old, traditional building and a new, modern development in the neighborhood.
Find a colorful wall, door, or mural and make it the subject of your photo.
Take a photo of a typical corner grocery store ('almacén' or 'verduleria').
Find a wine shop ('vinoteca') and take a picture of a shelf full of Malbec, Argentina's signature wine.
Capture a typical sidewalk scene in Villa Crespo - maybe people chatting, a dog being walked, or tables outside a cafe.
Take a close-up photo of an interesting texture you find in the neighborhood, like a peeling paint wall, a patterned tile, or a brick facade.
Find a quiet, peaceful spot in the neighborhood and take a photo that captures the calm.
Photograph a collection of interesting signs—shop signs, street signs, or advertisements.
Find a large, colorful mural—Palermo Hollywood is famous for them. Take a photo that captures its scale and creativity.
Photograph a stylish café. Focus on the design, the people, or the beautifully presented coffee.
The neighborhood is named for its concentration of TV and film studios. Find a building related to media production and snap a picture.
Find a boutique design or clothing store and capture a unique detail in its window display.
Take a photo in one of the neighborhood's green spaces or along a particularly leafy, tree-lined street.
Capture the buzzing atmosphere of a bar or restaurant in the evening. Focus on the lights and the energy.
Take a picture of a delicious-looking dish or drink from one of Palermo's many trendy eateries.
Take a selfie against a backdrop that screams 'Palermo'—a cool mural, a stylish cafe, or a beautiful street.
Find a cobblestone street and capture the contrast between the old-world charm and the modern, trendy businesses that line it.
Photograph a scene that shows the mix of architecture, from old 'casas chorizo' to modern apartment blocks.
Look for a 'pasaje' (a small alley or passage) and take a photo looking down it.
Find a 'cervecería artesanal' (craft brewery) and snap a photo of its sign or its taps.
Capture the lively scene of people enjoying food and drinks at the outdoor tables of a restaurant or bar.
Find a small, quirky piece of art—a sticker, a small stencil, or a painted utility box—and take a close-up shot.
Take a photo that focuses on the color palette of a particular street or building facade.
Spot a 'paseador de perros' (dog walker) with their pack of dogs, a common sight in Palermo. Take a photo of the happy hounds.
Find an apartment balcony overflowing with plants and flowers. Capture this small urban jungle.
Get a scoop from one of the area's many artisanal ice cream shops and take a picture before it melts!
